equipment.description: Primary nuclear reactor vessel for PWR systems.
Primary nuclear reactor vessel for BWR systems.
Large heat exchanger used to convert water into steam from reactor heat.
Large centrifugal pump for circulating primary coolant.
Mechanism to insert or withdraw control rods in the reactor core.
Component to maintain pressure in the primary coolant loop.
Heat exchanger to condense steam back to water after the turbine.
Removes moisture and reheats steam between turbine stages.
Preheats boiler feedwater using extraction steam.
High-pressure stage of the main steam turbine.
Low-pressure stage of the main steam turbine.
Converts mechanical energy from turbine into electrical energy.
High-head pump for emergency core injection.
Removes decay heat from the reactor core.
Circulates cooling water to auxiliary components.
Circulates water to cool the spent fuel pool.
Large globe or gate valve to quickly isolate the main steam line.
Safety valve to prevent overpressurization of the primary loop.
Regulates flow of feedwater to the steam generator or reactor.
Sprays water inside containment to reduce pressure during accidents.
Pressurized tank storing borated water for emergency injection.
Stores concentrated boric acid for reactivity control.
Stores large volume of borated water for refueling and emergency core cooling.
Maintains required volume and pressure in the chemical and volume control system.
Cools reactor coolant being letdown for purification.
Recovers heat from letdown flow to preheat charging flow.
Removes ionic impurities from reactor coolant.
Filters particulate matter from the primary coolant.
Combines hydrogen and oxygen in containment to prevent explosions.
Provides chilled water for safety-related HVAC systems.
Provides emergency AC power upon loss of offsite power.
Compresses air for starting emergency diesel generators.
Provides clean, dry air for pneumatic instrumentation and valves.
Provides final heat sink cooling from a lake, river, or ocean.
Measures neutron flux to determine reactor power level.
Monitors neutron flux during reactor startup.
Measures the flow rate of reactor coolant.
Measures water level in the pressurizer.
Measures coolant level directly in the reactor vessel.
Monitors pressure inside the containment building.
Measures pressure of the main steam lines.
RTD measuring the temperature of reactor coolant leaving the core.
RTD measuring the temperature of reactor coolant entering the core.
Monitors general area gamma radiation levels.
Monitors radioactivity levels in process streams.
Distributes power to large motors (e.g., reactor coolant pumps).
Distributes power to smaller 480V motors and equipment.
Assembly to control multiple low voltage electric motors.
Steps down voltage from the grid or main generator for plant use.
Provides uninterruptible DC power for critical controls and safety systems.
Converts DC battery power to AC for vital instrumentation buses.
Controls the speed of large AC motors for flow control.
